Conformance depends on a trusted Rust behavior oracle encoded as canonical fixtures. This bead is the tooling foundation used by fixture-capture streams.

Subtasks:

- Implement deterministic capture execution pipeline with stable scenario IDs and seeds.
- Provide capture CLI interfaces used by core/robustness/vertical fixture-family tasks.
- Document tooling usage and output contracts for downstream conformance/e2e stages.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Tooling can capture and emit schema-valid canonical fixture artifacts reproducibly.
- Outputs include provenance and version metadata.

 {"id":"bd-1md.10","title":"Implement canonical e2e scenario script suite with deterministic run-manifest artifacts","description":"Background:
The plan requires comprehensive e2e coverage with rich diagnostics; this bead is an aggregation checkpoint over dedicated e2e script families.

Subtasks:
- Integrate outputs from e2e family beads bd-1md.16 through bd-1md.18.
- Standardize script inputs (seed, scenario pack, profile, codec, resource class) and output locations.
- Emit run manifest per e2e invocation containing run_id, git rev, compiler/target, seed, scenario IDs, profile/codec matrix, semantic digests, and first-divergence pointers.
- Add triage-friendly first-failure summary output and rerun command snippets.

Acceptance Criteria:
- E2E scripts are one-command reproducible locally and in CI.
- Every failing e2e run writes complete logs and artifact manifests.
- Scripts are mapped to hard gates and release verification workflows.

 {"id":"bd-1md.11","title":"Implement structured test-log schema and artifact taxonomy for unit/invariant/conformance/e2e","description":"Background:
Detailed logging is only useful if schema and artifact taxonomy are stable and machine-readable.

Subtasks:
- Define JSONL log schema with required fields: ts, run_id, scenario_id, subsystem, profile, codec, seed, event_index, digest, status, and error metadata.
- Define artifact taxonomy and retention policy for stdout/stderr, trace snapshots, parity diffs, minimizer outputs, and replay bundles.
- Add validators that fail CI when required log fields/artifacts are missing.
- Document log interpretation playbook for rapid triage and regression localization.

Acceptance Criteria:
- All test layers emit schema-compliant logs.
- CI can detect malformed or incomplete artifacts automatically.
- Operators and maintainers can debug failures from artifacts without rerunning immediately.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-27T01:01:44.100625432Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-27T01:05:28.656288467Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["artifacts","logging","testing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1md.11","depends_on_id":"bd-1md","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-27T01:01:44.100625432Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1md.11","depends_on_id":"bd-1md.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-27T01:01:52.179021236Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"bd-1md.13","title":"Capture core semantic fixture families from Rust reference (lifecycle, outcome, budget, cancel, channel, timer)","description":"Background:
Core fixture capture must be decomposed to ensure complete semantic coverage and easier validation.

Subtasks:
- Capture fixtures for outcome joins, budget composition/exhaustion, and cancellation phases.
- Capture fixtures for region/task/obligation lifecycle legality and quiescence closure paths.
- Capture fixtures for channel reserve/send/abort and timer ordering/cancel semantics.
- Reconcile fixture families against extracted outcome/budget semantics (bd-296.16), channel/timer semantics (bd-296.17), finalization/quiescence invariants (bd-296.18), and invariant schema rows (bd-296.4).
- Attach provenance metadata and canonical scenario IDs.
- Define matching e2e invocation set and structured log expectations for this fixture family.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Core fixture set spans all kernel semantic units.
- Fixture outputs are deterministic and reproducible.
- Conformance runner can consume all captured families directly.
- Fixture families explicitly map to invariant schema rows and lifecycle/outcome/channel/finalization extraction sources.

 {"id":"bd-296","title":"EPIC: Spec Foundation and Plan-Space Canonicalization","description":"Background:
This project is explicitly spec-first. The C implementation must be derived from extracted semantics, not line-by-line translation from Rust source.

Goal:
Produce a self-contained canonical spec suite so implementation can proceed without consulting legacy Rust internals.

Must cover (non-negotiable):
- COMPLETE lifecycle semantics for region/task/obligation/cancellation
- outcome lattice and budget algebra
- forbidden-behavior catalog (must-fail matrix)
- machine-readable invariant schema
- scenario DSL and fixture contracts
- guarantee-substitution mapping (Rust guarantees -> C mechanisms)
- explicit exclusions/deferred surfaces and open decisions

Why this serves users:
- prevents semantic drift and accidental feature amputation
- enables predictable implementation and review
- allows independent auditors/future maintainers to understand intent without archaeology.

Cross-cutting quality contract:
- Child tasks must define and link unit tests, e2e script coverage (when behavioral), and structured log/manifest artifacts or explicit rationale for non-applicability.
- Completion evidence must remain compatible with bd-66l.9 enforcement and traceability index expectations.","status":"in_progress","priority":0,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-27T00:47:41.899905539Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-27T04:12:11.567704474Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["phase-0","phase-1","planning","spec"]}

 {"id":"bd-2cw","title":"EPIC: Runtime Kernel, Scheduling, Cancellation, Timer, and Channel Semantics","description":"Background:
Kernel behavior is where semantic fidelity is most fragile: ordering, cancellation, and queueing rules can silently diverge.

Goal:
Implement deterministic kernel loop and channel/timer semantics with rigorous fairness/starvation and correctness checks.

Must include:
- deterministic tie-break and event sequencing
- cancellation propagation checkpoints
- timer wheel semantics and O(1) cancel behavior
- bounded two-phase MPSC semantics

Why this serves users:
- reproducible behavior for incident analysis
- robust performance and correctness on both low-latency and constrained hardware.

Cross-cutting quality contract:
- Child tasks must define and link unit tests, e2e script coverage (when behavioral), and structured log/manifest artifacts or explicit rationale for non-applicability.
- Completion evidence must remain compatible with bd-66l.9 enforcement and traceability index expectations.","status":"in_progress","priority":0,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-27T00:47:42.106645851Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-27T04:20:12.160584077Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["phase-5","phase-6","runtime","scheduler"]}
